analysis.name: Amy Gottfried
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 229 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: One of my favorite professors at Michigan. She teaches clearly and makes lecture engaging. She provides tons of opportunities to ask questions in person and online, and always makes a point to be thorough, as well as make sure all students have the opportunity to ask their questions. Dr. G's class took heavy commitment but was extremely rewarding.

While Gottfried appears to be nice superficially, her exams are simply too difficult, and the class is even worse than orgo in that she teaches you extremely easy barebones stuff, and you literally teach yourself essentially everything else. There is really no point in paying for this course when I have legitimately taught myself all the material.

Gottfried is the only thing that made PChem bearable. She's one of the most committed professors on campus and wants to help her students. Her exams are extremely difficult, but she holds so many office hours to help us understand the material. This would probably be my least favorite course if any other professor taught it.

Dr. Gottfried is an incredible professor. Although this is debatably the hardest pre-med class at the University of Michigan, she explains everything in a way that makes sense. She seems to genuinely want her students to succeed. That being said, her exams are extremely difficult, but she does curve them. Would not take Pchem with any other prof.

Chem 230 is a tough class but Dr. Gottfried makes it enjoyable and provides lots of resources to aid you throughout the semester.   Piazza was very interactive and she would provide immediate feedback (within 24 hours). She also scheduled lots of office hours.  As long as you start the PREP 7 days+ prior to the exam, it's very doable.

analysis.name: Anthony Vecchiarelli
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I was disappointed that he read right off of the slides during lecture. Sometimes exam questions asked niche questions that were a sentence on one slide (“what type of engineering is this similar to”). I appreciated that he tried to crack jokes and showed us videos pertaining to what we were learning to try and connect all of the dots.

Dr. V is by far the best + most helpful professor I've had at UMICH! He genuinely cares about his students and is always happy to help, no matter the circumstance. His lectures are clear, and everything you need is on the slides. The exams are super fair and straightforward. Plus, he's just an overall kind and amazing professor. Highly recommend!

Lectures can get boring at times, but overall is engaging and clear. Exams and his "two of the above" questions are... not fun. Pay attention to every detail and you will do fine. He also provides extra credit for attending lecture.
I honestly really enjoyed Dr.V he tries to explain things in simpler ways and tries to crack jokes. I really enjoyed him because he is very inspirational.

I loved Dr. V! Exams can be a little tricky, but if you know the slides, you will do well. If you go to his office hours, he will tell you some of the content on the exam at the end so be sure to go! Great class though and I found it to be an easy A.

analysis.name: Audrey Bennett

review_highlights.comment: Second class... first class Typography where we designed compositions for her book - and now designing work for another member of her family in Branding. Prepare to experience her haughtiness and superiority with poorly planned classes.

Overall I got a good grade at the end of the class, sometimes things were not clarified in how they were graded. She gives lots of feedback if you ask for it and it's helpful. She also gives plenty of in class time to work on projects so take advantage of that. She is also very nice!

Took me several weeks to figure out that she didn't actually hate us all and could be quite friendly and encouraging, just with a very calm professional demeanor that came off as cold. She does things very by the book. Had some of the best critiques ever in her class, though. Mixed bag for me but really depends on if her teaching style is for you.

Audrey sets up her class by letting student figure out the assignments for themselves, which can be intimidating at first. Her humor is very dry and she can seem scary, but is actually extremely amicable and cares a lot for her students. She loves to interact and give feedback, and her critiques are incredible.
I learned more watching youtube videos - than from her "teaching".

review_highlights.comment: It is clear that Poniatowski knows a great deal about this subject, but often seems to forget that none of us have a degree in chemistry and rarely spends time explaining concepts. If you want to learn something from the lectures instead of googling everything and learning it all by yourself at home, then don't take it with him!
i ended up just skipping his lecture and going to bartley's instead for the majority of the time. he's funny but is not good at teaching chem unfortunately

Dr. Poniatowski is very energetic and entertaining. However, he doesn't hit everything that needs to be covered for the course and can get sidetracked. I liked the way he explained things, but I had to start going to Bartley's lectures to get all of the information. My grade got a lot better when I switched. Overall very nice guy.

Although he's a good lecturer, the format in which he teaches the class is unnecessarily confusing and annoying to follow. Hope he puts in effort in the future and makes handouts or anything for students.

review_highlights.comment: idk why ppl hate, i had keane and i would watch her lectures since I thought she made it easier to digest, whoever u have for stats youll be fine. stats is an semi easy but boring subject, do active approach, and check hw and case study with others and youll get an A easily dw

She's a great lecturer and especially helpful in OA and after class. Though STATS250 is an easy class by no means it is an 'easy A' imo. There's homework, MWrite, Labs, xyz... Just keep up with what's going on, try to get full marks on each homework and use office hours, you'll be fine. Lectures are recorded and you can go to any lecturer, dw.

You can decide if you want attendance, pre-lecture, and post-lecture work to count toward your grade. There are not a lot of study materials provided for the exams (you get 1 practice exam and a concept problem set).

This was my first time taking a Stats class in my entire life, and honestly, the content itself was easy to grasp, but she assumes everyone in the room has prior knowledge. After a few weeks, she toned it down and slowed to a much more manageable pace. She is very approachable, though, and going to office hours is SUPER helpful!

I don't understand the Romero hate. She is a great lecturer, even though the content can be dull. She is very nice and engaging and always willing to help and answer questions. Office hours are completely done with GSIs, though, but they are still very helpful and knowledgeable! I would definitely not skip out on Romero for stats.

analysis.name: Aaron Burch
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Aaron is a great instructor, chill/funny guy. He interacts well with students. This class really emphasizes the writing process, not just the essays themselves. Your grade is based off attendance/participation, essays, and some short writing assignments/annotated readings, pretty light workload. Definitely take this class with him if you can.

Really cool guy. Can be a tough grader. You have to be able to identify what he's looking for in essays or you'll do poorly, but it wasn't too hard to catch on. Overall, a great professor; I'd take a class of his again. And it's a REALLY good idea to go to office hours if you're struggling with a paper (saved me)!!

Would highly recommend this professor and class to anyone! Aaron Burch is very chill and engaging and you can tell he truly loves what he does and likes to get students excited about their writing. Took this class just because I needed ULWR and I feel like I learned a lot more than I thought I would. Not super tough, but you need to put in effort.

I've had Aaron Burch before for another class, too, and he was great in both of them He's big on group discussion, peer editing, and sharing work, so be ready for that!  If you like to not turn in tiny assignments, you may get in a bit of trouble because the small writing exercises for credit can add up. He's very comfortable and willing to chat!

Cool dude who I found really relatable. Wants class to participate. My section had a lot of people who slacked off so it made it awkward sometimes. There were a few moments where he literally just asked us why so few of us participated. Really passionate about writing and just wants students who care about it as much as he does was what I felt.

Very unclear on all assignments. She is rude when you ask for clarification and acts like you have no reason to be confused despite her vague prompts and directions. I actually had her for AAS 490 and would not recommend.

amal is a horrible teacher. there are no rubrics for assignments, she sends out vague emails then sends rubrics AFTER the fact. she grades arbitrarily & the group paper assignment at the end of the course is RIDICULOUS. readings are long & could be better chosen, she's very disorganized & often comes to class & office hours unprepared.

Realizing how good I had it compared to 402 with Cravino. review_highlights.comment: Bryan Roby is an absolute legend. Literally the smartest professor in his field. You will be suprised how much you learn about Judaism in North Africa. Roby is a professor I had the displeasure of ending up with. His grading criteria are incredibly unclear and his approach to teaching is "life doesn't give you a syllabus." His lectures are dependant on information he expects you to learn on your own (that he doesn't tell you about) and there is barely a common theme in the class.

Professor Roby is the best non-engineering professor I have had at the University of Michigan. Workload is light, the class is super revolved around participation and engagement which I love. He really cares for the students and he makes you realize that the main goal for this class isn't to get an A but actually learn the Judaic content.
Professor Roby is very understanding and really is just looking for people to engage in class discussion. If you just participate in class you are almost guaranteed an A. Solid class.
One of the best professors I have ever had. Really caring and I really think he is one of many few professors who truly cares for their students and realizes we have lives outside of class.

Professor Burke is a fantastic instructor and provides excellent in-class teaching that correlates to real-world experiences in production. This was my favorite professor of my first year of college, he is so caring and understanding. I went through a very rough time mentally during his class and missed alot of lectures and he still made sure i understood everything we were doing. Love this guy so much

Michael Burke was one of the best professors I have had at UMich. He is the coolest guy and taught us so much about the industry through his own experience and by producing ourselves. Best professor I have had at school thus far. Always helpful and available whenever you need it. Understanding when things go wrong. Offers great feedback and teaches the content incredibly. I'd give more than 5 stars if I could.

Grading is mostly weighted by 2 essays (with revisions) plus attendance. P.S. you may learn to juggle!

Professor Bardolph is genuinely one of the best professors that the school has to offer. Also an extremely sweet lady and clearly cares for her students!

Dr. Bardolph is amazing! She made the lectures interesting and enjoyable. I really enjoyed my time in her class and the things we discussed have helped me a lot. She is very friendly and always willing to talk about literally anything!

Prof Bardolph is so sweet and cares so much about the well-being of her students! She barely gave any work outside of class. Class is very very easy and not much is missed if you skip lecture.

Very light workload and engaging lectures made her class the highlight of otherwise tough days for me. She assigns work qualitatively with succinct but thoughtful guidelines I found nice. Hands down the perfect FYWR class you could take.

analysis.name: Mara Bollard

This course is a good way to gauge your interest in cognitive science.

Bollard was a great professor! She made lectures pretty easy to understand, and she was very passionate on cog sci! Your grade is based on lecture quizzes (which we discuss in class so it's pretty much easy perfect scores), 2 exams and a final, and 5 homeworks (best out of 4). Honestly, just study well & you'll be good. Take this class w her !!

Honestly stopped caring about this class after I P/F but Mara seems super sweet and you can tell she knows her stuff. Sometimes things can be a bit unclear, but for the most part she's decent! Save your time - don't use the textbook at all. I survived this class by networking with others who were able to explain problems. Look at the workbook for sure, some questions come from there.

Don't take 401 with Proulx if you have a choice. He barely gives examples, and when he does, they are far from what's on his tests. To make matters worse, his recommended textbook is useless. It is the first textbook I have seen without common examples. I really hoped the textbook would help prepare me for tests but it's as useless as the lectures.

I want to be fair. Proulx can be funny and he can really helpful during office hours. But I can't deny the fact that this class is so stupidly tough because he makes it so. There's absolutely no need to blow through material so quickly while teaching proofs and other things that just needlessly complicate material. 401 shouldn't be bad, but it is.

analysis.name: James Casey Jr.

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Casey was nice, accessible, and explained concepts clearly. However, he tended to misspeak sometimes which made things confusing. The final exam was very difficult. 80 questions with intense application-based questions that required a lot of thinking outside of what we learned in class. 4th exam had a lot of spelling errors and didn't look like it was revised.

This class is really hard for some reason. Exams often ask unfair questions that contradict what we learned in class. He is pretty good with dropping questions/giving partial credit. However, the time I spend studying for the class is not representative in the grading at all.

Good lecturer. The class is content heavy so the exams are difficult. You get extra credit from submitting questions on piazza. Has ocassional choose all that apply questions that are difficult. Starts class right at the start time with an iclicker question. iclicker is graded. Class has case studies done in groups before every exam.

I don't really get the hype. I just feel like everything is all over the place when he lectures and stuff is smushed together. Some of the exam questions make no sense it's mind boggling. I sense a lack of care too when it comes to going over stuff that's heavy on the exam.

analysis.name: Colleen Van Lent
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 33 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: (SI 101 used to be called 106) Colleen is the best and cares about her students. shes reasonable, understanding and would always give extensions on our assignments. Readings are helpful since lectures can be fast but many did fine without. 3 exams, 1 makeup date, 1 final project(group or alone), 3 'HWs', weekly quizzes and lecture activities(easy).

Colleen is an extremely kind professor, she always makes sure no one is confused and questions are answered. As far as teaching in class, she assumes you know a lot so definitely stay on top of readings if you actually want to understand what she is saying. She offers a lot of extra credit at the end of the semester so your grade will be ok.

Several extra credit opportunities. If you do well on exam 1 and 2 and do the rest of your assignments, you can take a 0/100 on exam 3 and still get an A (what I did). She's super nice and caring. Amazing prof. Would take again.

Course could be more organized, but overall it's not that bad. You might have to work a bit harder for a few assignments, but if you're a CS major taking it as an elective (like me), the workload should be much lower than what you're used to. Colleen and the GSIs are nice and try their best to help out too.

SI339 shift to CPDs was a disaster. The class was chaotic, unorganized, and confusing for both students and staff. OH were unhelpful, as GSIs and TAs were always confused themselves. I spent more time decoding assignments than learning, focusing solely on meeting deadlines rather than understanding the material. I don't recommend this class AT ALL!

analysis.name: Charles Severance

review_highlights.comment: He is one of the best Teacher I have seen and one best mentor. May Gol always keep him happy

He knows what he talks about. I enjoyed being able to work on the homework tutorials on my own time after I was finished with other prioritized homework. I learned a lot. SI 504 comes in clutch since we use the terminal a lot as well.

I took the Python classes on Amazon/video. hey! i am one of the student who is taking coursera classes. the series on python is amazing and the best i have seen so far! awesome knowing this class and thank you a lot for providing us the opportunity to learn online, especially for those of us who do not have privilege to pursue a formal computer class  Thank you again!!

analysis.name: Henry Cowles

Once a week we did collaborations in lecture, and I really enjoyed these- these mostly involved designing a product or an ad related to our topic for the week.

Professor Cowles is amazing. He truly cares about his students and his lectures are fascinating. He does lecture with little words on the slides, so it's important to listen, but he really is very knowledgeable. Would 100% take this class again!

Very dreamy; sometimes hard to think about math, but then he proves great theorems out of nowhere.

Dr. Zieve is a very knowledgeable mathematician but sometimes gets carried away in lecture. He makes random comments about people in class and their grades but overall he's a nice person. Just beware because he assumes students know some of the things he explains but does care about how his students do and grades fast. Not the best or worse in 116.

Dr. Zieve does a lot for students and is pretty much always available outside of class. He shares a lot of useful test prep and strategies, and as long as you read the textbook chapters beforehand, his lectures are easy to understand and very helpful. Overall a very knowledgable mathematician and intersting guy. I'm super glad I took his section.

A pretty great professor overall. It's obvious he's genuinely passionate about what he teaches, which makes learning from him a lot less dull. At times he may go a bit fast or go off on a tangent, but always asks if anyone has questions. Extremely accessible outside of class, and puts in regrade requests for exams on behalf of students.

analysis.name: George Zhang
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Great proffesor in terms of caring and knowledge. However when it comes to teaching a class and interacting in order to further learning a strong weakness is there. Very hard to learn and unserstand the key points for exams.
Zhang is so smart but he should NOT be a professor. Some people should realize their strengths and weaknesses.

Dr. Zhang knows genetics very well, he just does not know how to teach it. The class required to memorize formulas, so one would think the highlighted formulas in the lecture slides are the ones to memorize . However, this is not the case, any formula is fair game. I recommend taking the class during winter, or any time when he does not teach it.

The only reason I understood the material was because I went to GSI office hours. He does not explain concepts well in class and doesn't set clear expectations for exams. States that you don't need to know details but his exams are all details. Glazes over lecture problems he expects students to know, doesn't prepare you for difficult HW problems

The problem is not George, it's CBE. They try to fit so much into the curriculum but students don't actually retain the information. He was ok, still better than Lee McClain imo. Grading scale consisted of: 15% homework (6) 25% midterm 15% Quiz (exam 2) 45% Final I had to teach a lot of the content myself but he curves heavily (rightfully so).

review_highlights.comment: The half of genetics taught by this professor was brutal. Lectures didn't tend to be helpful since the material is dense and the speed with which we were expected to learn it was approaching breakneck. Didn't provide nearly enough resources to achieve the level of knowledge expected from us on the exams, which were a little insane content-wise.

Dr. Zhang's half of genetics was extremely difficult. Obviously learning styles are very different but I found his lectures to be very hard to follow and the exams to be harder than many of the other classes I have taken here at UM. Don't underestimate this class and assume that you will be tested on very small-scale topics!

He's knowledgeable, but he didn't seem interested in teaching and sometimes confused himself on his own practice questions during class. The exams in his half of the course were notably much more difficult. I highly recommend using Problem Roulette and exams from previous years to study.

Genetics is a very hard class, and I didn't feel like Dr. Zhang helped to make it easier. He frequently misspoke during lecture and struggled to explain some concepts to us. We often got to discussion entirely confused, only to be saved by our GSI's explanation. He's a nice guy, but not a great professor.

Genetics is a difficult class, and Dr. Zhang was not very good at explaining concepts. It sometimes felt like he couldn't explain certain questions, and he would sometimes get the questions wrong in class. Tests were also very difficult!

analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students

review_highlights.comment: I hated this class so much, often times Leo would go on long tangents that were more in depth than the course curriculum. If you haven't taken physics before I would recommend that you do Toms online lectures or take an intro course to this intro course. I wish I had done that because I think I might've liked physics.

Clearly cares a lot about his students and very knowledgeable. Lectures are theory-heavy, probably hard to understand without prior physics knowledge. He uses calc 3/4 concepts sometimes, again hard to understand without prior knowledge. Great prof, would recommend if you've taken an AP physics class before!

Super knowledgeable guy, not good at teaching the material. Often times skips many steps in explanations and goes into depth for hard concepts that are not needed to understand the material. Exams are tough but lots of extra things for notes in this class.

Prof. Zayas is kind and willing to help students outside class. He posts lecture notes before class and they are complete and sufficient to do well in the course. Homework was not too difficult and exams were open book and open notes. He would also give practice exams from previous years that were somewhat helpful. He is also a generous grader!

Dr. Zayas is a good professor. His lectures can be a little hard to understand, but he posts several pages of notes afterwards that are quite useful. He has a ton of demos that are both fun to watch and educational. The studio version of physics 140 is definitely the better version. The studios really help in material understanding.

Exams are disgusting, which are written by another professor. Lost my passion for calc here

This class is definitely the hardest class I have taken at Michigan, but this professor explains things well. I definitely recommend doing practice problems in the book along with the additional homework because the tests were super hard. The tests my year were only made by one professor (not mine) and were unnecessarily difficult.
